rulings.Case Title: ALDINO SANI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 10, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT JOHNSON CONTROLS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. The questioning of plaintiff is less probative than that deemed not to shift the burden in Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433, 1439. In addition, defendant did not submit the interrogatories that were propounded. Absent the questions, the Court is unable to conclude that the responses are factually devoid. Further, plaintiff's declaration is admissible based on having his recollection refreshed. That testimony creates a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PETER H GOSS VS. MARGARET SCHEINMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 10, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT MARGARET SCHEINMAN, MELVIN SCHEINMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The Court declines to rule on causes of action previously addressed. Defendant has not complied with CCP 437c(f)(2) and has not shown why those issues could not have been addressed at the time of the initial motion. The burden on the Court and the parties is compounded by the shortened briefing schedule and defendants failure to comply with page and type size limitations. The motion is denied as to Berlin based on his declaration that creates a triable issue of fact as to his interest in the property. There is a material dispute of fact as to what plaintiff means by out of pocket costs. Paragraph 22 of the Goss Declaration includes "creating, operating and maintainiing the restrauant." Capital expenditures incurred in creating and maintaining the restrauant, based on the representation of a renewal period, would appear to be recoverable damages for the alleged breach. Although plaintiffs got the space they bargained for at the time of the initial lease, the square foot shortage is related to the fair market rental value going forward and, in turn, plaintiffs ability to recover its investment expenditures as contrasted with operating expenses. The Court makes no determination on plaintiffs' right to cover all sums spent on the restrauant on the theory that but for the representations, they would not have entered into the transaction. The square footage claims are not barred because there is a factual dispute as to when plaintiffs learned of the discrepancy. It appears that the promissory estoppel claim is barred by the Court's prior ruling that the parties had merely an agreement to agree and contract claim related to the 1900 square foot dispute is not amenable to a promissory estoppel claim because it is baed on the parties' contract. For the reasons stated, the Court denies defendants' motion except as to the promissory estop

rulings.Case Title: SARAH STROTHER VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 10, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. The Court finds that, as a matter of law, the defect is trivial. The Court relies on defendant's facts 10-14, 16, 20-34, and Exs. A, B & C to the Radell declaration. The Wexler declaration does not create a triable issue of fact for he does not adress the causes of the accident, plaintiff's failure to look. The motion based on design immunity is denied for failure to shift the burden of producing evidence. The Court has sustained plaintiff's objections 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, and 9 and the identical objections to the same evidence related to other parts of the motion. As a consequence, defendant has not shown that the records relied up by declarants Golden and Radell are admissible as an exception to the hearsay rule. The foundational facts required by Evidence Code section 1280 have not been shown. As to Golden, there are insufficient facts to provide a foundation for his testimony regarding events that occurred before Golden became an employee. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC. VS. JEFFREY LAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Deposit Of Funs With The Clerk Of The Court, For Discharge Of Pltf And For Payment Of Costs And Reasonable Atty'S Fees From The Deposited Funds
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 10, 2008, line 8. PLAINTIFF STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S Motion For Deposit Of Funds With The Clerk Of The Court, For Discharge Of Plaintiff And For Payment Of Costs And Reasonable Attorney's Fees From The Deposited Funds. The motion is granted. Under CCP section 386 (c) plaintiff did not need a Court order to deposit the funds. The parties' papers demonstrate that these are competing demands for the funds. The related bankruptcy case required plaintiff to do more than would ordinarily be required. The Court awards $7,000.00 to cover reasonable fees and costs of plaintiff. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: HOLLY BRATT RN VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Pltf'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 10, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, WADE NOGY, COLLEEN MCKEOWN, DEBRA RUCKERT AND TERRI NORIEGA'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend on each of the grounds asserted. Plaintiff must plead facts that enable each defendant to understand the factual and legal basis for claims alleged as to them. Each cause of action is to state only one legal claim. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN STATE BORING & PIPE JACKING, INC., A VS. AFFHOLDER, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 10, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT AFFHOLDER, INC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration is granted. Plaintiff's contract with defendant incorporates the arbitration clause of the contract between defendant and the prime contractor and eliminates any ambiguity in paragraph 13.9, a written document that, at a minimum, reflects the core agreement betweet plaintiff and defendant. The parties, by their coduct have waived the condition of mediation. =(302/PJM/VC)
